meaning: adj.有些粘的,缺乏教养或风度的,俗气的
examples: The movie had a really tacky ending.
Test to see if the finish is tacky, and if it is, leave it to harden.
It is a very tacky whirligig with a little man who chops wood as the wind blows the propeller.
Many are pretty tacky: full of plastic flowers, fake stained-glass windows and doll's-house pews.
"Back then it was related to something a bit dressy or tacky, " he said.
